chain.description: The deployments.Create() method in kubernetes-client-go sends an HTTP POST request to the Kubernetes API server. It uses the REST client to post the Deployment object to the /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{ns}/deployments endpoint.

The API server's deploymentStrategy.PrepareForCreate() is called during Deployment creation to clear non-user-settable fields and set server-side defaults. This is part of the REST storage layer's create path.
The storeTxnWrite.Put() function in etcd persists the serialized Deployment object to the MVCC key-value store. It increments the revision and generates a watch event for controllers to act on.

text: Kubernetes Deployment creation end-to-end flow: Step 1 — Client library (sg-evals/kubernetes-client-go, kubernetes/typed/apps/v1/deployment.go): The deployments.Create() method constructs an HTTP POST request to the API server's REST endpoint using the typed client. Step 2 — API server (kubernetes/kubernetes, pkg/registry/apps/deployment/strategy.go): The deploymentStrategy.PrepareForCreate() function validates and prepares the Deployment object, clearing client-settable-only fields before persisting. The deployment is handled by the REST store in pkg/registry/apps/deployment/storage/storage.go. Step 3 — Storage (etcd-io/etcd, server/mvcc/kvstore_txn.go): The storeTxnWrite.Put() function stores the serialized Deployment in etcd's MVCC store, incrementing the global revision and emitting a watch event that triggers the deployment controller.
